description: Direct factual lookup — single page answers the question

pages.name: API rate limits
pages.content: Free tier: 100 req/min. Pro: 1000 req/min. Enterprise: unlimited (SLA-backed). Rate limit headers: X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset. Exceeding returns 429.

pages.name: Webhook setup
pages.content: Configure webhooks in the dashboard under Settings > Integrations. Provide a URL and optional secret. Events: message.created, workspace.updated, persona.sleeping.

task: What is the API rate limit for a Pro tier account?

pages.name: Production deployment checklist
pages.content:
1. Merge to main via PR (requires 2 approvals)
2. CI passes all checks
3. Tag the release: git tag v{major}.{minor}.{patch}
4. GitHub Actions builds and pushes Docker image
5. Terraform apply in production workspace
6. Run alembic upgrade head on production DB
7. Verify health endpoint: GET /health
8. Monitor error rate for 10 minutes

pages.name: Staging deployment
pages.content: Staging deploys automatically on every merge to main. No manual steps required. Staging DB is reset weekly.

task: What are the steps to deploy a new release to production?
answer_hint: 8-step process from PR merge to health check verification

description: Multi-source synthesis — answer requires two pages

pages.name: Python SDK documentation
pages.content: Install: pip install octopus-sdk. Authentication: pass your mc_ API key to OctopusClient(api_key='mc_...'). Key methods: client.push_event(), client.inject_context(), client.search_pages().

pages.name: Node.js SDK documentation
pages.content: Install: npm install @octopus/sdk. Authentication: new OctopusClient({ apiKey: 'mc_...' }). Key methods: client.pushEvent(), client.injectContext(), client.searchPages(). All methods return Promises.

task: How does authentication differ between the Python and Node.js SDKs?
answer_hint: Both use mc_ API keys but different constructor syntax

pages.name: ADR-012: asyncpg over SQLAlchemy
pages.content:
Decision: Use asyncpg directly instead of SQLAlchemy ORM.
Status: Accepted.
Context: We need high-performance async database access with full control over queries.
Decision: Raw asyncpg with manual SQL. SQLAlchemy Core for Alembic migrations only.
Consequences: No auto-generated queries. Developers write SQL directly. Better performance and debuggability.

pages.name: Database connection pooling
pages.content: The app uses asyncpg.create_pool() with min_size=2, max_size=20. Pool is initialised at startup and torn down on shutdown.

task: Why did the team decide not to use SQLAlchemy ORM?
answer_hint: Performance, explicit control, better async support; SQLAlchemy Core still used for migrations

description: Status check — find current state of a feature

pages.name: Auth0 migration status
pages.content:
Status: IN PROGRESS (as of 2025-Q1)
Completed: Backend JWT validation, Auth0 SDK integration, BFF proxy.
Pending: Social login testing with Google/GitHub, enterprise SSO (SAML), invite-link flow for Auth0 users.
Target completion: 2025-Q2.

pages.name: Stripe billing integration
pages.content:
Status: COMPLETE
Launched 2024-Q3. All payment flows live. Webhook handlers deployed.

task: What is the current status of the Auth0 migration and what is still pending?
answer_hint: In progress; BFF done, social login and SAML pending

description: Graph-expanded context — answer is one hop away

pages.name: Curation pipeline overview
pages.content: The curation pipeline runs nightly. It consists of three stages: event aggregation, LLM summarisation, and relation extraction. See the Sleep agent page for implementation details.

pages.name: Sleep agent implementation
pages.content: The sleep agent calls _llm_curate() with recent history events. The LLM returns a JSON payload with create_notes, update_notes, and extract_relations actions. The executor applies each action to the notebook. Actions are idempotent.

task: What actions can the curation LLM take when it runs?

pages.name: AGENT PERSONA: Aria
pages.content: You are Aria, a helpful assistant for the Octopus platform. Always respond concisely. Escalate to human support if the user reports a billing issue. Your workspace ID is ws_abc123. Always maintain a professional tone.

pages.name: Billing FAQ
pages.content: Q: How do I upgrade my plan? A: Visit Settings > Billing > Upgrade. Q: Can I get a refund? A: Contact support within 14 days.
